codeReview.summary: 18 well-structured tests covering all branches of both getProgressColor and getConfidenceColor, plus non-numeric input safety. All assertions from VAL-COLOR-001 through VAL-COLOR-008 are fully covered. Tests are clean, follow project conventions, and use appropriate assertion styles (toBe for exact string matches, not.toThrow for safety checks). No mocking needed as these are pure functions.

summary: The test-colorHelpers feature is well-implemented. All 8 validation assertions (VAL-COLOR-001 through VAL-COLOR-008) are fully covered by 18 test cases organized in two describe blocks. getProgressColor tests cover zero progress (VAL-COLOR-001), full progress (VAL-COLOR-002), partial progress with boundary values 1, 25, 50, 99 (VAL-COLOR-003), and non-numeric inputs undefined/null/NaN (VAL-COLOR-004). getConfidenceColor tests cover zero confidence (VAL-COLOR-005), low confidence 1 and 2 (VAL-COLOR-006), medium confidence 3 (VAL-COLOR-007), high confidence 4 and 5 (VAL-COLOR-008), plus non-numeric safety. Code quality is good: descriptive test names, proper use of toBe for exact CSS value assertions, and not.toThrow for input safety. The worker followed procedure with no deviations. All 18 tests pass.
